The country's parliament also elected another vice-president from a military-aligned party and a third will be chosen by the military. A parliamentary vote to select which of the three will be elevated to president is expected later this week.

Industry contamination of soil with toxic metals exposes failure to translate science into policy
items.description: Eve Irvine is pleased to welcome Dr. Jagannath Biswakarma, Honorary Senior Research Associate at the University of Bristol. As an environmental scientist, his work focuses on understanding how human activity reshapes the natural systems we depend on, often in ways that remain invisible until their consequences surface in our bodies. He examines the growing global exposure to toxic heavy metals such as cadmium, arsenic, lead, and mercury, substances that, while naturally present in the Earth, are increasingly concentrated through industrial and agricultural processes.

items.description: A group of children reenacted a funeral with their doll as they played together in a displacement camp in Gaza.

items.description: Israel blocked Jerusalem’s Catholic cardinal from marking Palm Sunday at the Church of the Holy Sepulchre, sparking an international outcry that led Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to reverse the ban for ‌the remainder of Holy Week.

items.title: Gulf allies privately tell Trump to keep fighting until Iran is decisively defeated
items.description: Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman has told White House officials that a further defanging of Iran's military capabilities and clerical leadership serves the long-term interest of the Gulf region and beyond

items.title: Trump says he has 'no problem' with Russian oil tanker bringing relief to Cuba despite blockade
items.description: President Donald Trump said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.

items.title: In historic Monaco visit, Pope Leo XIV calls for justice and peace
items.description: Pope Leo XIV makes first papal visit to Monaco since 1538, urging the wealthy microstate to use its influence for justice, peace and the common good.

items.title: Amnesty flags FIFA World Cup human rights risks
items.description: The human rights group has sounded the alarm about risks faced by visitors to the 2026 FIFA World Cup. It goes as far as to describe the situation in the United States as a "human rights emergency."

items.title: Russian students duped into signing contracts with military
items.description: University students are being recruited to serve in drone units fighting Ukraine. They are offered lucrative contracts and promised they won't be sent to the front. Reports suggest those promises are not always kept.

items.title: First stop, the Moon. Next stop, Mars? Why Nasa's mission matters
items.description: From a race with China to lunar discoveries, the US is investing time, effort and money to head to the Moon - and beyond.

items.title: Egypt's former top diplomat Nabil Fahmy named as new chief of Arab League
items.description: Arab foreign ministers on Sunday unanimously named former Egyptian foreign minister Nabil Fahmy the Arab League's new Secretary General.

items.title: Kris Jenner's image spreads in Chinese social media good luck trend
items.description: Hundreds of thousands of posts have been shared in the past three days as users hope for prosperity

items.title: WTO talks in Yaounde end in deadlock over e-commerce customs duties
items.description: World Trade Organization talks in Cameroon ended early on Monday without agreement after deep divisions over e-commerce duties blocked a deal.

items.title: State firms in black but Milei presses ahead with privatisation plan
items.description: Four of the six state-run companies President Milei and his economy minister want to sell closed out 2025 in surplus; Privatisations appear to be government’s key path to raising dollars and reinforcing reserves. Leer más

items.title: Argentine diplomat Virginia Gamba removed from UN secretary-general race
items.description: Argentine diplomat Virginia Gamba removed from list of candidates for UN secretary-general after Maldives withdraws backing for her candidacy; Boost for fellow Argentine Rafael Grossi; Days ago, Chile pulled support for ex-president Michelle Bachelet’s candidacy. Leer más

items.title: Why Chinese tech companies are racing to set up in Hong Kong
items.description: Mainland firms are using the territory to test products and as a springboard for global expansion.

items.title: News24 | G7 allies press Rubio on US-Iran plans
items.description: G7 allies were on Friday pressing US Secretary of State Marco Rubio for clarity on American plans for Iran almost one month into the war in the Middle East, with concern also intensifying over the extent of cooperation between Russia and the Islamic republic.

items.title: Ukraine: Top EU diplomats mark 4 years since Bucha massacre
items.description: Brussels' chief diplomat and several EU foreign ministers are in Ukraine to mark four years since one of Russia's most infamous atrocities. The Bucha massacre, just weeks into the full-scale war, shocked the world.

items.title: Don’t agree with my husband on every issue: Usha Vance
items.description: Usha Vance said the 2028 presidential campaign was not a priority in her conversations with her husband, who was more focused on the mid-term elections due in November

items.title: Pope Leo condemns using God to justify war in Palm Sunday mass
items.description: The pope has said God rejects the prayers of those who wage war and have "hands full of blood." Leo has also prayed especially for Christians in the Middle East during a Palm Sunday Mass.

items.title: Argentina designates Mexico's CJNG cartel a ‘terrorist organisation’
items.description: Milei government adds Jalisco New Generation Cartel to registry of terrorist organisations, following in footsteps of Washington, and reaffirms “commitment to fight against organised crime.” Leer más

items.title: Middle East war live: Iran attacks tanker at Dubai Port, Chinese vessels transit Strait of Hormuz
items.description: An Iranian attack on a Kuwait-flagged oil tanker at Dubai Port on Tuesday caused a fire, but no injuries or oil spill, according to Emirati authorities. Meanwhile China on Tuesday confirmed that three Chinese ships were allowed to transit the Strait of Hormuz in recent days. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.

items.title: Caputo cheers ‘all-time high’ as INDEC data shows rise in economic activity in January
items.description: Milei and Caputo cheer news that economy grew in line with expectations; Activity in first month of the year up 1.9% year-on–year, by more than 8% from two years ago. Leer más

items.title: Crypto fuels drone purchases in Russia and Iran: Report
items.description: While most drone purchases are made using traditional financial rails, procurement ‌networks ⁠are increasingly intersecting with the blockchain, the public digital ledger ⁠on which cryptocurrencies are based, Chainalysis found

items.title: Iran confirms Revolutionary Guard navy chief Alireza Tangsiri’s death
items.description: The IRGC praised his efforts, particularly in helping Iran maintain a chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Argentina's economy grew in line with expectations in January
items.description: Argentina’s economy expanded slightly in January following a quarter of weaker-than-expected growth, says INDEC national statistics bureau. Leer más

items.title: Fuel rations and free buses: How countries are responding to rising oil prices
items.description: Governments around the world have introduced measures to limit the impact of price increases.

items.title: Zelenskyy visits Doha as Ukraine, Qatar sign defense pact
items.description: Ukraine and Qatar have agreed to cooperate on high-tech projects and share battlefield expertise on countering missile and drone threats.

items.title: Dozens killed in deadly gold mine attack in South Sudan
items.description: The perpetrators remain unclear, with conflicting accounts emerging. A security source suggested the involvement of forces linked to Riek Machar, while his representatives have denied responsibility and instead accused the army loyal to President Salva Kiir.

items.title: 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scores 15-ball fifty for Rajasthan Royals
items.description: Indian teenage prodigy smashes his third IPL half century as Rajasthan routs Chennai Super Kings in the IPL.

items.title: Two suspects in Liam Payne drug supply case released pending trial
items.description: Two men accused of supplying drugs to Liam Payne released from house arrest but still face trial in Buenos Aires. Leer más

items.title: US reopens embassy in Venezuela after seizing Nicolas Maduro
items.description: The State Department said it had formally reopened its embassy in Caracas after seven years. The US seized President Nicolas Maduro in a military raid in January and has since reestablished diplomatic ties.

items.title: Rescuers try to find 27 people believed to be on raft drifting in Indonesian waters
items.description: The passenger boat departed Taliabu port in North Maluku province just after dusk Sunday (March 29, 2026). It was bound for Kema in North Sulawesi province before sinking in the northern waters of Taliabu Island

items.title: News24 | US meeting with ‘very reasonable’ Iran leaders says Trump, hints at taking Kharg Island
items.description: US President Donald Trump said the US and Iran have been meeting “directly and indirectly” and that Iran’s new leaders have been “very reasonable”, as more US troops arrived in the region and Tehran warned it will not accept humiliation.

items.title: Legal experts push back on removal of 'good character' evidence in NSW
items.description: Legal figures are pushing back against sweeping changes to New South Wales sentencing laws, warning proposed reforms to abolish "good character" references could undermine long-standing principles of justice.

items.title: Japan Post Insurance to acquire 2.9% stake in Britain's Ashmore Group
Germany hosts Syria's Al-Sharaa despite human rights issues
items.description: Germany is keen for Syrian refugees to return home. The visit by interim Syrian President al-Sharaa highlights shifting policies on repatriation and regional stability — despite accusations of human rights violations.

items.title: LeBron James records triple-double as Lakers dominate Wizards
items.description: Led by James's 125th regular-season triple-double, the LA Lakers destroyed the Washington Wizards for their 49th win.

items.title: At 40, Lady Gaga isn't done revolutionizing pop music
items.description: As iconic as she is outlandish, Lady Gaga has left quite a mark on the world of music. Here's how she reinvented pop — and reached millions by opening up about her trauma and illness.

items.title: Why the 'ultimate' Barbie fan event left visitors wanting a refund
items.description: Barbie fans who paid up to $656 for an "unforgettable experience" were met with a concrete-floored warehouse and flimsy pink cardboard cut-outs.

items.title: Spain closes airspace to US warplanes involved in war on Iran
items.description: Spain has taken another step in rejecting the US-Israeli war on Iran, denying US warplanes the use of its airspace.

items.title: Crude prices whipsaw on mixed signals from Iran war
items.description: Oil prices searched for direction on Tuesday amid conflicting events related to the Iran war. While US President Donald Trump's threats that the US would obliterate Iranian energy infrastructure if the Strait of Hormuz remained closed led to an increase in prices, a later report saying Trump is seeking an end to the war brought them down. Also in this edition: inflation in France ticks up because of higher energy prices and Australia threatens legal action against social media giants.

items.title: Nestle says thieves stole 12 tons of KitKat chocolate bars
items.description: "We've always encouraged people to have a break with KitKat," the company said, "but it seems thieves have taken the message too literally."

items.title: Pentagon denies that Hegseth’s broker sought investment before Iran war
items.description: US Department of Defense demands retraction of report alleging broker sought multimillion-dollar investment for Hegseth.

items.title: Touadera sworn in for third term as Central African Republic president
items.description: Central African President Faustin-Archange Touadera on Monday pledged to buttress security and peace as he was sworn in for a third term in one of the world's poorest and most volatile countries

items.title: Rain, storms kill 45 people in Afghanistan and Pakistan
items.description: The weather has already prompted the closure of several highways, officials in central and eastern Afghanistan said, with further rain and storms forecast for March 31

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: News24 | New blow as UK’s Sarah Ferguson loses freedom of York city
items.description: York officials unanimously stripped Sarah Ferguson of her “Freedom of the City” title over her ties to Jeffrey Epstein, marking another blow to her public image.

items.title: Flavio Bolsonaro inches ahead of President Lula in Brazil election poll
items.description: Son of of imprisoned former president Jair Bolsonaro, would take 47.6% of the vote compared to Lula’s 46.6% in a run-off, according to new AtlasIntel survey. Leer más

items.title: Morocco's Tangier port prepares for spike in traffic driven by Gulf crisis
items.description: Located in northern Morocco on the Strait of Gibraltar, Tangier is a major stopping point for Europe-bound container ships

items.title: Whale stranded off Germany's coast gets stuck again
items.description: The unlucky humpback was spotted stranded on a sandbank in the Bay of Wismar, less than two days after being set free.

items.title: Sacked Brisbane oil exec accuses Shell of fraud cover-up
items.description: Kent Quinlan's lawyers told the Supreme Court the oil company sacked him because he allegedly uncovered widespread financial fraud.

items.title: News24 | Iran's response to US peace proposal expected Friday, source says
items.description: Iran’s response to a US peace proposal aimed ‌at ending the war in ⁠the Middle East is expected on Friday, according ⁠to a source briefed on the matter.

items.title: After sex abuse claims, activists and lawmakers rethink Cesar Chavez Day
items.description: California has renamed Cesar Chavez Day to Farmworkers Day after the labour-rights icon was accused of sexual abuse.
